page: Amazon Web Services r6idn.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance offers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data places this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score is 30967.14, while the Geekbench multi-core score reaches 14084.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1657.33 GB/sec, dropping to 149.78 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Database operations are supported by a Passmark database score of 14776.38 and a Redis SET throughput of 31.23 million operations per second.

While the instance demonstrates strong performance in small-scale LLM text generation, yielding 307.77 tokens per second for a 135M model, it exhibits significant performance degradation on larger models. The LLM inference speed drops to a weak 6.2 tokens per second for prompt processing and a poor 2.25 tokens per second for text generation on a 70B model, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. Consequently, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, and caching layers rather than large-scale AI model inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services r6idn.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD, and a 50 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute and memory tests, with a Passmark CPU score of 36566.42 and a Redis SET throughput of 31.23 million operations per second. While it achieves a strong text generation speed of 307.77 tokens per second on a small 135M LLM, performance drops to a poor 2.25 tokens per second on a 70B model due to the absence of GPU acceleration. This instance is designed for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and high-throughput network tasks.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86 platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local NVMe storage, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

256.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.
Bundled 1900 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers 31.23 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and high-throughput network workloads.
